mechanism_narrative: Rising real GDP signals broad economic expansion, boosting corporate earnings and leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 20-day forward S&P 500 returns are negative in more than 50% of quarters with positive GDP growth (qoq annualized > 2%).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate rises above 5%, tightening monetary policy increases discount rates and depresses equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward S&P 500 returns are positive in more than 60% of instances when FEDFUNDS exceeds 5.0.

mechanism_narrative: Strong labor demand, indicated by rising payroll employment and falling unemployment simultaneously, boosts consumer confidence and spending, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward S&P 500 returns are negative in more than 50% of months when both conditions hold.

mechanism_narrative: Once CPI exceeds 200 (index level), inflation fears prompt the Fed to tighten, raising rates and hurting equities; the mechanism flips from neutral to negative in this high-inflation regime.
failure_condition: 20-day forward S&P 500 returns are positive in more than 50% of quarters when CPIAUCSL > 200.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production signals strong manufacturing activity and economic momentum, leading to positive short-term S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward S&P 500 returns are negative in more than 60% of periods with positive INDPRO month-over-month growth.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts above 2,000K units indicate strong residential investment and consumer confidence, which historically precedes positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 20-day forward S&P 500 returns are negative in more than 50% of quarters when housing starts exceed 2,000K.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in retail sales and GDP reflects robust consumer and aggregate demand, boosting corporate earnings and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward S&P 500 returns are negative in more than 50% of months when both conditions hold.

mechanism_narrative: When unemployment is below 5%, the labor market is at full employment, leading to wage pressures and Fed tightening, which historically depresses S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward S&P 500 returns are positive in more than 60% of months when UNRATE is below 5.0.
